title: In Search of a Free Campsite
summary:
Our next planned stop was the town of Auburn. Auburn is home to the famous Western States 100 mile endurance race, so I was excited to go check out the trails and also spectate a different race that would be happening that weekend. We drove to the local trail system and I went for an awesome run while Helen went for a hike down to a lake. Our luck ended there as we spent the next five hours driving around the forest service roads of the area looking for a place to camp for free. Between muddy roads and campgrounds that hadn't opened yet for the season, we struck out completely. We spent a good while looking for any campground that had availability and found one near Folsom Lake. Happy to finally have a place to stay for the night we headed down and arrived at a great camp spot. I made beans and rice bowls for dinner and then we watched more Beef with Abby on the projector in our van. In the morning, Helen and I took Maya down to the lake and she was very excited to fetch for a stick and get a nice swim in. After checking out of camp, Helen had to do some work, so while she was doing that, I went for a nice run around the lake Natoma, which had a great paved path for running.

-Colin
